businessSummary.oneLiner: Government and enterprise AI data analytics platform, transforming from a core U.S. defense/intelligence partner into a commercial AI powerhouse
businessSummary.description: Palantir builds software platforms that analyze and integrate complex data. Since the launch of AIP (AI Platform), the commercial segment has seen explosive growth. Q4 2025 revenue reached $1.407B (+70% YoY), with U.S. commercial revenue at $507M (+137% YoY). Shares closed at $143.50 on 3/6 (-9.92%) as Iran peace negotiation signals eroded the defense premium that drove the previous six-day rally.
howTheyMakeMoney: Government contracts: Software licenses and maintenance for U.S. DoD, CIA, NSA and national security agencies
Commercial segment: AIP-powered enterprise AI analytics platform subscriptions, U.S. commercial revenue +137% YoY
International government: NATO allies and Five Eyes partner government contracts
Professional services: Implementation, training, and consulting
keyProducts: AIP (AI Platform): Connects LLMs to enterprise data for AI-driven analytics
Gotham: Government and defense data analysis and decision support
Foundry: Enterprise data integration and analytics platform
Apollo: Software deployment and management automation
Maven: U.S. DoD AI operations support system

keyMetrics.name: P/E Ratio (TTM)
keyMetrics.interpretation: Still extremely elevated despite the selloff, reflecting AI growth premium

keyMetrics.name: Forward P/E
keyMetrics.interpretation: Earnings growth expectations continue to support the valuation

keyMetrics.name: PEG Ratio
keyMetrics.interpretation: Expensive relative to growth. Sustained growth rate is critical

keyMetrics.name: Operating Margin
keyMetrics.interpretation: Strong profitability befitting a software platform

keyMetrics.name: FCF
keyMetrics.interpretation: Strengthening cash generation with ample reinvestment capacity

growthDrivers.title: Middle East military conflict fueled defense AI rally (+4.06%)
growthDrivers.description: Escalating Middle East tensions drove a defense stock rally. Palantir led alongside Lockheed Martin on rising Gotham/Maven demand expectations.

growthDrivers.title: Rosenblatt raised target to $200 (from $150)
growthDrivers.description: Rosenblatt Securities maintained Buy and raised its price target by 33% to $200 on 3/3, reflecting accelerating defense AI demand.

growthDrivers.title: Motley Fool: 2026 guidance could supercharge growth
growthDrivers.description: Record Q4 results and AIP commercial acceleration are reshaping the 2026 outlook. Total contract bookings reached $4.26B.

growthDrivers.title: AIP commercial adoption accelerating: U.S. commercial revenue $507M (+137% YoY)
growthDrivers.description: AIP boot camps are driving enterprise customer conversion. Q4 U.S. commercial revenue of $507M grew 137% year-over-year.

competitiveAdvantage.summary: Top Secret/SCI government clearances, 20-year defense and intelligence relationships, and AIP-driven enterprise AI platform expansion form the core moat.

moats.description: Top Secret/SCI clearance. Extremely difficult for competitors to replicate.
Gotham/Foundry are deeply integrated into customer data infrastructure. Replacement costs are prohibitively high.
AIP boot camps rapidly expanding enterprise customers. More usage drives better models.

competitors.name: Snowflake
competitors.detail: Data cloud platform competing in enterprise data analytics

competitors.name: Databricks
competitors.detail: Lakehouse platform competing in AI/ML workloads

competitors.name: Microsoft (Azure AI)
competitors.detail: Azure AI services targeting government and enterprise markets

competitors.name: C3.ai
competitors.detail: Enterprise AI platform, though significantly smaller than PLTR

recentNews.headline: PLTR drops 9.92% as Iran peace negotiation signals deflate defense premium
recentNews.significance: NYT reports Iran intelligence conveyed willingness to negotiate with CIA. Defense stocks sold off sharply

recentNews.headline: Motley Fool asks: Is Palantir the best buy in March?
recentNews.significance: Wall Street returning to Buy list after 38% pullback. Valuation debate continues

recentNews.headline: Palantir emerges as the Logic Layer of the Agentic AI revolution
recentNews.significance: AIP positioned as core infrastructure for the agentic AI era

recentNews.headline: Bloomberg: Palantir back on Wall Street's Buy list after 38% plunge
recentNews.significance: Bloomberg highlights PLTR as a re-entry opportunity amid defense AI demand

recentNews.headline: Trump administration bans Anthropic from government contracts, benefiting PLTR
recentNews.significance: Anthropic's exclusion strengthens Palantir's monopoly in government AI market

analystOpinions.notableComment: 18 analysts maintain Buy consensus. The selloff widens upside to +35% vs. consensus $194. Rosenblatt targets $200, Citigroup leads at $255. Bloomberg highlights the re-entry opportunity after a 38% decline.

risks.title: Extreme valuation at P/E 228x
risks.description: Even after the selloff, P/E remains at 228x. Any growth deceleration could trigger further significant correction. PEG of 2.33 also indicates overvaluation.

risks.title: Geopolitical risk cuts both ways
risks.description: Shares surged on the Iran conflict and plunged on peace signals. Defense dependency is a double-edged sword.

risks.title: CEO insider selling
risks.description: Persistent stock sales by CEO Alex Karp weigh on market sentiment.

risks.title: Intensifying commercial competition
risks.description: Growing competition from Snowflake, Databricks, and Microsoft Azure AI.

risks.title: Share dilution
risks.description: Stock-based compensation drives 4.67% annual share count growth, diluting existing shareholders.

buyReasons.title: Selloff creates opportunity: +35% upside to consensus
buyReasons.rationale: The 9.92% drop on Iran peace news brings shares to a 35% discount versus the 18-analyst average target of $193.78.

buyReasons.title: U.S. commercial revenue exploding at +137% YoY
buyReasons.rationale: AIP boot camps are accelerating enterprise customer conversion. Growth engine beyond defense remains intact.

buyReasons.title: Anthropic government ban strengthens PLTR monopoly
buyReasons.rationale: Trump administration's exclusion of Anthropic from government contracts solidifies Palantir's position in government AI.

buyReasons.title: Net cash of $6.95B provides financial fortress
buyReasons.rationale: Debt-free balance sheet with ample cash limits downside risk.

overallOpinion: PLTR plunged 9.92% to $143.50 as Iran signaled willingness to negotiate peace, erasing the defense premium that drove six consecutive days of gains.
The defense narrative faded, but U.S. commercial revenue growth of +137% YoY and the Anthropic government contract ban remain intact as secular tailwinds.
At P/E 228x the valuation is still stretched, yet the selloff opens a 35% discount to the analyst consensus target of $194.
Trading below both the 50-day and 200-day moving averages with RSI at 44.5, a technical bounce becomes plausible as the stock approaches oversold territory.

x.hook: PLTR -10%, does peace kill AI stocks?
x.text: $PLTR dropped 9.92% to $143.50. Iran signaled willingness to negotiate with the CIA, and the defense premium evaporated instantly. Six straight days of gains wiped out in one session. (1/4)
thread: But defense isn't the whole story. U.S. commercial revenue is up 137% YoY, and Trump's ban on Anthropic from government contracts actually strengthens Palantir's monopoly in government AI. (2/4)
The valuation problem persists. P/E ratio of 228x means you're paying 228 times earnings for this stock. Still, at $143 vs. the analyst consensus of $194, that's a 35% discount. (3/4)

Trading below both the 50-day and 200-day moving averages with RSI at 44, a technical bounce is plausible. Is the post-war selloff the real buying opportunity? investory.kro.kr/stock/PLTR/analysis/2026-03-06 (4/4)

Below the 50-day average, RSI at 44. The question: is this a war-premium unwind or a genuine entry point? investory.kro.kr/stock/PLTR/analysis/2026-03-06

witty.hook: PLTR: war makes it soar, peace makes it crash
witty.text: PLTR investors this week: Monday 'War is great for AI stocks!' Friday 'Wait, peace talks?' Iran sends one signal to the CIA and $PLTR drops 10% to $143.50. Six days of gains, gone in hours. (1/4)
thread: Here's the irony though. Take away the war premium, and you still have a company growing commercial revenue 137% a year. Trump even kicked Anthropic out of government, handing Palantir more market share. (2/4)
P/E at 228x and calling it a 'discount' sounds absurd, but at 35% below the $194 analyst target, it's technically the cheapest it's been in weeks. The word 'cheap' doing heavy lifting here. (3/4)

The real question: are you buying Palantir the defense stock, or Palantir the AI platform? Your answer determines whether today was a panic or a gift. investory.kro.kr/stock/PLTR/analysis/2026-03-06 (4/4)

empathy.hook: PLTR holders, how are you feeling tonight?
empathy.text: $PLTR -10%. Six days of gains. Gone. Just like that. Iran says 'let's talk' and $143.50 is all you're left with. If you're holding, I know today stung. (1/5)
thread: The emotional whiplash is real. You watched it climb for almost a week, felt validated, maybe even bragged a little. Then one headline erases everything. That's the cruelty of momentum trades. (2/5)

But step back from the screen for a moment. Commercial revenue is still growing 137% a year. The Anthropic ban is a real competitive advantage. The business fundamentals haven't changed in 24 hours. (3/5)
Yes, P/E at 228x is expensive by any measure. But 18 analysts see $194, which is 35% above today's close. That's not nothing. The selloff widened the gap between price and consensus. (4/5)

Before you panic sell, ask yourself why you bought in the first place. If it was the war trade, maybe it's time to reassess risk. If it was the AI platform thesis, today might just be noise. Take care of yourselves. investory.kro.kr/stock/PLTR/analysis/2026-03-06 (5/5)

witty.hook: PLTR: the stock that loves war and hates peace
witty.text: Week starts with missiles, PLTR goes up 10%. Week ends with diplomacy, PLTR goes down 10%. $143.50. The defense premium giveth and the defense premium taketh away. But here's the thing: strip out the war narrative and you still have 137% commercial growth, an Anthropic-free government monopoly, and 18 analysts saying $194. P/E 228x is wild, but 35% below consensus is hard to ignore. War stock or AI stock? Your call. investory.kro.kr/stock/PLTR/analysis/2026-03-06

empathy.hook: To every PLTR holder who watched today's -10% in disbelief
empathy.text: Six straight days up, then one headline from Iran and it's all gone. $PLTR -9.92%, $143.50. I get it, today hurts. But the 137% commercial growth didn't disappear. The Anthropic ban still benefits Palantir. Analysts still see $194, now 35% away. The question isn't whether today was painful. It was. The question is whether your investment thesis was built on war headlines or AI fundamentals. If it's the latter, breathe. This might just be noise. investory.kro.kr/stock/PLTR/analysis/2026-03-06
